title: Determine the exact value of R(B21, B21)
micro_paper_assessment: The exact 85-vs-86 claim would still be a micro-paper if closed, but the current output is only a sharp structural slice: any 85-vertex witness must be an srg(85,42,20,21).
generalization_signal: The common-neighbor summation and Cauchy-equality collapse appear reusable for other diagonal one-gap book Ramsey residues on 4n+1 vertices.
candidate_theorem_slice: Any graph on 85 vertices whose graph and complement both avoid B21 must be strongly regular with parameters (85,42,20,21).
next_action: Verification should check whether the reduction to srg(85,42,20,21) is already known and whether that conference graph is known to exist or not; if still open, the next solve step is construction/nonexistence strictly inside the conference-graph lane.
